_comment: Eval cases for the information-gain skill. 'underspecified' problems should yield several genuinely high-value questions; 'well-specified' ones should yield few or none (the skill must not manufacture questions). Bounds feed the deterministic calibration check; the adjudicator judges quality.

cases.problem: Build a service to sync USAW events into our team calendar.
cases.notes: Source format/API, auth, sync cadence, dedup, and which calendar are all unstated.

cases.problem: Set up monitoring and alerting for our microservices.
cases.notes: Stack, SLOs, on-call routing, hosting, and data-residency constraints are unknown.

cases.problem: Add search to our internal documentation site.
cases.notes: Corpus size, ranking-quality bar, infra budget, and access control are unstated.

cases.problem: Write a Python function reverse(s: str) -> str that returns s reversed. Pure function, standard library only, no I/O and no external dependencies; treat the input as a sequence of Unicode code points and reverse them as-is; reverse('') must return ''.
cases.notes: Fully specified. A good analysis surfaces few or no high-value questions (calibration test).
